rulings.Case Title: ANDREW STEWART LENNON VS. LELAND HAROLD VAN WINKLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 22, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFF ANDREW LENNON'S Petition To Compel Arbitration is denied. Van Winkle did not consent to or ratify the original arbitration agreement that petitioner wants enforced. The court acquired jurisdiction when defendant was served with the underlying complaint before the letter was issued by JAMS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: B.A., Minor, Guradian ad Litem, York Liu VS. SF UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al ***SEALED PER COURT ORDER***
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 22, 2010, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Granted with 10 days leave to amend to plead facts demonstrating oppressive, fraudulent or malicious conduct to support punitive damages. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: B.A., Minor, Guradian ad Litem, York Liu VS. SF UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al ***SEALED PER COURT ORDER***
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 22, 2010, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 9th and 10th causes of action. The 9th cause of action is predicated upon a violation of the Unfair Insurance Practices Act and the insured cannot maintain a private right of action. Activities covered by the UIPA cannot support a violation of B & P Code section 17200. Cross complainant does not contest demurrer as to 10th cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREI ROMANENKO VS. STANISLAV SHABALIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default; Declarations Of Stanislav, Kerre A. Renn And Stella Stapletaon In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 22, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT STANISLAV SHABALIN'S Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default. Denied. Defendant fails to show lack of actual notice of the lawsuit. The evidence establishes that defendant avoided service of the lawsuit and his actions constitute inexcusable neglect. Plaintiff was diligent in trying to obtain defendant's address. (CCP section 473.5). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: TEAMSTERS LOCAL 853
rulings.Calendar Matter: Noticeof Motion And Motion On Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 22, 2010, line 12. PETITIONER TEAMSTERS LOCAL 853'S Motion On Petition To Compel Arbitration. Granted. Here, a broad arbitration clause is employed that allows for arbitration of any allegation that the "City has violated, misapplied or misinterpreted a term or condition" contained in the MOU. Petitioner alleges that respondent violated the MOU for failing to reassign the two truck drivers and not paying them the appropriate compensation. The timing of an employment decision and the number and identity of the employees affected by the decision is subject to bargaining. See Fire Fighters Union, Local 1186 v. City of Vallejo (1974) 12 Cal 3d 608. There is a strong public policy in California of adopting arbitration for employment disputes. The MOU does not expressly exclude the current dispute from arbitration, but does explicitly exclude others. =(302/CWW)
